Shanto, Najmul Hussain BAN vs pace

Bangladesh M · LHB · How to exploit — pace plan

innings with ≥25% of team
3-yr 23%

Summary

  • A solid, aggressive batter — vulnerable to swing.
  • Plan for pace: bowl good length outside off, gets it to seam in.
  • They get out most often to balls that are full and outside off (4.8 dismissals/100).
  • Most often out caught behind (40% of dismissals); most often to right fast (14).

Common themes

  • left-hand bat, averaging 28.2 at a strike rate of 53.2.
  • Carries a big share of the batting — 14.6% of team runs.
  • Scores mainly through the off side (50%).

Strengths

  • Main scoring shot is the work/nudge (42% of runs).
  • Scores unusually little off the cut vs pace (3% of runs vs 7% typically).
  • Starts securely — their dismissal rate barely drops once set (1.6 early vs 2.2/100); early pressure alone won't buy their wicket. Early runs come mainly off the work/nudge (43%).

Weaknesses / how to get him

  • Most often out caught behind (40% of dismissals); most often to right fast (14).
  • Vulnerable to the good length (averages 19 there vs 28 overall; 1.7 dismissals/100).
  • Struggles outside off (16 avg, 3.3 outs/100).
  • The ball that seams in hurts them (avg 15 vs 42 when it holds its line).
  • Most dangerous ball: full and outside off (4.8 dismissals/100, avg 20).
  • His drive is high-risk (false shot 30%, 2.6 outs/100).

Match Impact (share of runs)

Makes 14.6% of their team's runs over a career (typical innings 12.6%), and 3.9% of all runs in their matches; carries the innings (≥25% of the team's runs) 23% of the time.

Suggested Fields (the stock field ± their evidenced deviations)

Each field starts from the stock template for that bowler type & phase (orthodox because it works), then makes at most three changes — each one earned by a trigger in their game clearing the cohort P75 bar (do they lap, cut, pull, score square, edge early?). Change rows are highlighted with their stat; Stock rows carry the orthodoxy line. The read line backtests the changes against the pure stock field. Early = first 30 balls; Set = once in. Drawn from behind the bowler (bowler bottom, striker top); off side is on the right.
Field vs right-arm pace
Early — first 30 balls · 391 balls
Pure stock field. Nothing in their game clears the deviation bar (cohort P75) against right-arm pace, so the orthodox field stands — that is the read.
FielderStock/ChangeWhy they're there
Slip 1StockStandard stock position.
Slip 2StockStandard stock position.

Backward pointStockStandard stock position.

Mid-offStockStandard stock position.
Mid-onStockStandard stock position.
Fine legStockStandard stock position.
Third manStockStandard stock position.
Floating fielder: They score only 4% of their runs where mid-off stands — the spare run-saver to move as the game asks.
Once set · 453 balls
Pure off-side / square stock field — Scores square and through the off (55% vs 27% leg) — an extra fielder square on the off, one leg-sider out.. Nothing in their game clears the deviation bar (cohort P75) against right-arm pace, so the orthodox field stands — that is the read.

Third manStockStandard stock position.
Floating fielder: They score only 5% of their runs where mid-on stands — the spare run-saver to move as the game asks.
Bouncer plan
He pulls a fair amount (P68) — the bumper can buy a top-edge. One slip, a catcher in front of square, and the two legal leg-side riders (deep backward square + deep fine leg).
FielderStock/ChangeWhy they're there
Slip 1ChangeOne slip kept for the top / outside edge.
Short legChangeCatcher in front of square for the fend or glove off the hip.
GullyChangeThe steer or fend that flies squarer of the wicket.
PointChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
CoverChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Mid-offChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Mid-onChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Deep backward squareChangeDeep behind square for the top-edged pull.
Deep fine legChangeDeep behind square for the hook.
